meta_title: For Founders: debugging with AI to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Debugging with AI to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on your MVP. Learn to leverage AI debugging and Antigravity to transform technical roadblocks into profitable, market-ready SaaS products.
content.answer_block: The paralysis of having 'no project ideas' is usually a lack of technical leverage. By using AI to debug existing open-source tools or legacy codebases, you uncover hidden inefficiencies. These inefficiencies are your business opportunities. Turn debugging into product discovery by automating the fix and wrapping it in a SaaS UI.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. You’re sitting there, burning cycles on 'what to build,' while the real gold is buried in the friction of existing, broken workflows. Every bug in an open-source library is a market signal—a sign that a user is stuck and willing to pay for a streamlined solution.

We stop guessing and start reverse-engineering. By leveraging AI-driven debugging, we turn technical debt into a roadmap for your next MVP. This isn't just about fixing code; it's about extracting value from the cracks in the ecosystem to build something that actually solves a burning problem.

steps.title: Identify Friction Points
steps.description: Find an open-source project in your target niche with a high volume of unresolved issues. Focus on usability or installation bugs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the top 20 most active but unresolved GitHub issues for [Repo Name]. Categorize them by 'Complexity' and 'User Impact'. Identify which ones represent a recurring friction point for non-technical users.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure State
steps.description: Use Claude Code to recreate the bug in a local environment. Don't just fix it; understand the architectural reason for the failure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Based on the identified bug, write a minimal reproduction script that fails consistently. Explain the underlying architectural bottleneck causing this.

steps.title: Automate the Fix
steps.description: Develop a robust, automated patch that handles the edge case. This patch is the core of your potential product.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the identified module to handle the exception gracefully and improve performance by 20%. Ensure the fix is modular and reusable.

steps.title: Wrap in a SaaS Interface
steps.description: Convert the fix into a micro-service or a simple web interface that users can interact with via API.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a FastAPI boilerplate that exposes the fixed logic as a RESTful endpoint. Add secure authentication and rate-limiting.

steps.title: Validate Market Demand
steps.description: Deploy your solution as a 'solution-as-a-service' and monitor if users struggling with the original issue adopt your fix.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a landing page copy that highlights the specific bug solved, focusing on the time-saved metric for the end user.

pro_tips: Treat every GitHub issue with a 'help wanted' label as a potential customer waiting for a feature.
Use AI to write the 'How to use' documentation for your patch; if the AI can't explain it simply, your product is too complex.
Always build the fix as a standalone library first; it makes your SaaS pivotable if the initial product idea fails.
Monitor the pull request comments of the original project to see what users complain about most after a fix is implemented.
Focus on 'Time-to-Value' metrics; if your AI-debugged solution saves 5 minutes, charge for the 5 minutes, not the code.
resources: GitHub API for issue scraping: https://docs.github.com/en/rest
content.next_steps: Once your micro-service is live, share it directly in the comments of the GitHub issues you solved to drive immediate, high-intent traffic to your MVP.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in a vacuum and start solving real-world technical pain points that people are already searching for. Your next SaaS isn't a pipe dream; it's a bug fix away. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
